ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Sep-05-2013 Continued To Jun-05-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Marine Engineering And Supply Company
Off Calendar Jul-16-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 514, Judge: Donald Sullivan, Clerk: Yolanda Mazariegos, CSR: Sonia Wong, #11288.

Kimberly Chu, Esq., appearing for Plaintiff Larry Martin.
Order Granting waiver of Witness Requirement of Hearing on Application for Default Prove-up, on file.
No appearance by defaulted defendant(s) Marine Engineering and Supply Company.
Default of Defendant(s) has been duly entered.

After Evidence and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff and against Defaulted Defendant(s) as follows:

APPEARANCES:
Lisa L. Oberg, Esq. of McKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE (415-267-4175), appearing for defendant / moving party Metalclad Insulation Corporation
Anne T. Acuna, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Larry Martin

RULING:
DEFENDANT METALCLAD INSULATION CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corp.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiff failed to present evidence creating a triable issue whether he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Unibestos insulation) attributable to defendant. Defendant's objections to the portions of plaintiff's declaration stating that laggers performed work on the Drum are sustained and defendant's remaining objections to plaintiff's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Cohen's declaration are sustained. Prevailing party is to prepare an order (in compliance with CRC 3.1312). Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)
